hook.text: This is the most overlooked endometriosis symptom. An Endo nurse explains why so many people miss it.
hook.emotional_mechanism_raw: surprise / intrigue — 'most overlooked' implies something hidden that the viewer may be unaware of, prompting curiosity
hook.target_audience_raw: people with endometriosis or those suspecting endometriosis, especially those whose fatigue is not being linked to their condition

main_claim.text: Fatigue / lethargy is the most overlooked symptom of endometriosis, affecting 95% of patients in a small study.

supporting_explanation.summary: The nurse describes a self‑conducted survey of 100 patients in which 95% reported fatigue. She contrasts this with other common symptoms (70% dyspareunia, 80% painful periods) and explains that fatigue is often dismissed because it can be attributed to many other causes. She hypothesises that fatigue stems from a low‑grade, ongoing inflammatory process, using a metaphor of a slow drip in a ceiling.

funnel_rationale: The video raises awareness of a lesser‑known symptom (fatigue) and broadens the understanding of endometriosis beyond painful periods. It does not offer diagnosis or treatment steps, nor does it directly promote a consultation service, making it top‑of‑funnel educational content.

cta.requested_action_raw: click the link in the bio
cta.value_exchange_raw: access to Liz, an Endometriosis Clinical Nurse Specialist who offers online consultations

secondary_raw: endometriosis symptom dismissal; inflammatory model of fatigue
